name: Text utilities
description: Control over how text behaves when it runs out of horizontal space within its container.

families.name: truncate
families.description: Shorthand for setting all necessary properties to enable ellipses on text overflow.

families.name: white-space
families.description: Controls how browser layouts handle text wrapping, sequences of spaces, and raw line breaks.
guidance: Use 'white-space-pre' or 'white-space-pre-wrap' to ensure rendered code indentation stays aligned.

families.name: text-overflow
families.description: Dictates how hidden, overflowing text is signaled to users.
guidance: Inline elements ignore width constraints, preventing text-overflow from working. Set to inline-block or inline-flex.

This property also does not work if not combined with overflow-hidden and white-space-no-wrap (use .truncate as a singular class to set all three properties at once with text-overflow set to 'ellipsis'

families.name: word-break
families.description: Specifies whether or not the browser should insert line breaks within words when text overflows its container.
guidance: word-break:break-word is deprecated. Avoid using it and implement overflow-wrap: break-word or overflow-wrap: anywhere instead

families.name: overflow-wrap
families.description: Tells the browser whether it can split an unbreakable string of text (like a massive word or a long URL) into a new line.
guidance: Requires the container to have a setting that permits wrapping.
Use 'overflow-wrap-break-word' to keep long unbroken links from distrupting layouts.

families.name: hyphens
families.description: Controls how words are hyphenated when text wraps across multiple lines.
guidance: If you use hyphens-auto, you must set the lang attribute on your HTML element (or the <html> root).
Use the Unicode character ­ to dictate where manual hyphens will occur if needed.

families.name: text-wrap
families.description: Controls how text breaks and flows inside an element.
guidance: Use text-wrap-balance on headings where you want to keep an equal amount of words per line.
text-wrap-pretty keeps word orphans from occuring.
text-wrap-stable prevents jumping when text is being editted.
